---
title: "How Basketball Analysts Track and Visualize Shots"
author: "Damilola Sule"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    incremental: true
    embed-resources: true
---

## The Game Has Changed

![](images/game-changed.png){width=100%}

::: notes
Basketball analytics has changed modern shot selection. Teams now emphasize shots near the rim and three-pointers because those shots are usually more efficient than long midrange attempts.

But in order to identify these patterns, teams first needed a way to track every shot on the court.
:::

---

## How Teams Track Every Shot

![](images/shottracker-balls.png){width=70%}

Systems like **ShotTracker** collect shot-location data, player movement, and real-time court coordinates.

::: notes
 It uses sensors and tracking systems around the court to collect movement and shot data in real time. It is especially used in college basketball and team analytics settings.
:::

---

## Turning Coordinates Into Shot Charts

![](images/maxey-shot-chart.png){width=100%}

Analysts transform tracking data into shot charts, heat maps, efficiency zones, and player tendency models.

::: notes
Once the data is collected, analysts can visualize where players are most efficient and how different lineups affect shot quality.

These visuals help teams identify offensive strengths, weaknesses, and patterns.
:::

---

## From Visualization to Strategy

![](images/sga-shot-chart.png){width=100%}

Shot analytics helps teams build defensive game plans, optimize lineups, and improve offensive spacing.

::: notes
Teams use these visualizations to decide how to defend star players, which lineups create efficient shots, and where players should attack.

So the charts are not just visuals. They influence actual strategy.
:::

---

## Main Takeaway

> Basketball analytics is not just about counting shots — tracking systems transform movement and shot data into strategy, lineup decisions, and winning basketball.

::: notes
Modern basketball analytics connects technology, visualization, and strategic decision-making.
:::
